summary: @digicroz/typed-api-kit relays caller payment API tokens and customer PII through an author-controlled Google Apps Script proxy
details: The package exports a payment-gateway client whose `paymentGateways.pay0Pg.createOrder` function hardcodes its base URL to a Google Apps Script web-app endpoint owned by the package author (https://script.google.com/macros/s/AKfycbxbz7BQ.../exec?url=https://pay0.shop/api). When a caller invokes the advertised API, the function POSTs the caller-supplied pay0.shop merchant API token (`user_token`), customer mobile number, order ID, amount, and redirect URL through the author's Apps Script before reaching pay0.shop. The README documents direct pay0.shop integration and does not disclose the relay, so callers using the advertised API silently leak their merchant credentials and customer PII to the Apps Script owner, who can log every transiting payload. A secondary deception signal compounds the risk: package.json's description advertises a 'Type-safe OneSignal push notification client' with onesignal/push-notifications keywords, while the only shipped surface is the payment-gateway proxy code — increasing the chance a developer installs this without realizing what it does.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
